title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for complexity, profiling, rendering budgets, and responsiveness and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for representative performance and responsiveness report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.
Measure generation, solving, state conversion, drawing, callback delay, startup, save, and package behavior separately.
Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Capacity Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Capacity Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 6644 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.
